name: ZenML
tagline: Write ML and AI pipelines once, run them on any infrastructure backend
seoTitle: ZenML - Portable MLOps Pipelines for Any Stack
metaDescription: ZenML is an open-source MLOps framework for building portable, reproducible ML and LLM pipelines that run on any orchestration backend, from local to cloud.
overview: ZenML is an open-source MLOps framework for writing ML and AI workflows as pipelines that run on any infrastructure backend. You describe your logic once as Python pipelines and steps, and ZenML handles containerizing the code, tracking each run, and abstracting away the underlying infrastructure.

It is built for ML and AI engineers working on traditional ML use cases, LLM workflows, or agents in a company setting. The same pipeline can run locally during development and on a remote stack such as SageMaker, GCP Vertex, or an orchestrator of your choice in production, without rewriting the code.

Within the MLOps and compute orchestration space, ZenML acts as a portable layer over your existing tools. It integrates with systems like MLflow, LangGraph, and Langfuse, and tracks runs with metrics, logs, and metadata so you can iterate on experiments in development and production.
features: Write workflows as Python pipelines and steps that run on any infrastructure backend (called stacks)
Automatically containerizes and tracks your code so runs are reproducible
Tracks individual runs with metrics, logs, and metadata for observability
Abstracts away infrastructure complexity so the same pipeline runs locally or in the cloud
Integrates with existing tools like MLflow, LangGraph, Langfuse, SageMaker, and GCP Vertex
Client-server architecture with an integrated web dashboard for inspecting pipelines and runs

gettingStarted.intro: You can install ZenML, initialize a repository, and connect to a server in about five minutes.

steps.heading: Install ZenML
steps.body: Install ZenML with server capabilities. Plain `pip install zenml` installs a slimmer client only.

steps.heading: Initialize your repository
steps.body: Run this inside your project to set up ZenML.

steps.heading: Start or connect to a server
steps.body: Start a local server or connect to a remote one.

steps.heading: Explore the quickstart example
steps.body: The repo ships an examples folder; the quickstart demonstrates core concepts like pipelines, steps, artifacts, snapshots, and deployments. See the docs for your first AI pipeline.

useCases: Run the same ML training pipeline locally during development and on a remote cloud orchestrator in production without changing the code
Operationalize LLM workflows and agentic loops with run tracking, logs, and metadata
Standardize and reproduce ML experiments across a team using tracked, containerized pipelines
Connect existing tools like MLflow, LangGraph, SageMaker, or GCP Vertex behind one portable pipeline layer
